marketSummary.methodology: Pricing bands triangulated from public service pages, LinkedIn Jobs salary bands, and proposals seen in RFPs. Reliability score rates ops maturity + delivery predictability (0 = gig-worker chaos, 10 = Fortune 500 agency rigor). Threat rates how often a prospect shortlists them alongside us — high/medium/low.

priceMatrix.note: From the deck 'Pricing Blueprint' slides. All SGD. Where we sit is bolded in the admin UI.

competitors.name: Mutant Communications
competitors.ourAdvantage: Half the price; faster turnaround thanks to the AI layer. We keep monthly reporting; they revert to quarterly executive decks.
competitors.ourGap: Less PR / earned-media bandwidth. Weaker on industry-analyst relationships.
competitors.positioning: Full-service PR + comms for scale-ups and regional launches.
competitors.battlecard: If a prospect name-drops Mutant, position us as the execution arm — same ops quality, no premium PR surcharge. Ask: do they actually need PR, or are they buying PR as a proxy for 'looking credible'?
objections: Mutant has bigger name brands → we have 15yr Elitez parent + AI-augmented ops stack + group-shared overhead, not an agency-rent retainer
Mutant does earned media → we quote a PR specialist as add-on only when needed

competitors.name: Construct Digital
competitors.ourAdvantage: Retainer-native; ROI reported monthly, not at project end. Our 30-day notice beats their 6-month-minimum.
competitors.ourGap: Smaller portfolio of named enterprise logos.
competitors.battlecard: Lean on retainer bundling vs. their one-off project quotes that tend to balloon. Ask prospect: 'Last Construct project — did the final invoice match the proposal?'
objections: Construct has enterprise clients → fine, but you're an SME
Construct does bigger sites → we include web updates in retainer; we don't need to sell it as a project

competitors.name: Verz Design
competitors.ourAdvantage: We lead with outcomes; they lead with deliverables. Website is one thing we do — not the thing.
competitors.ourGap: Verz owns the SG web-build niche; their portfolio is deeper on site design.
competitors.battlecard: If prospect eyes Verz for a site build, quote our Website build add-on ($2K-5K) + Core retainer — comparable total, ongoing ROI instead of project wrap.
objections: Verz builds prettier sites → fine, but can they report lead cost after 6 months?

competitors.name: 2Stallions
competitors.ourAdvantage: Entry tier ($1K) matches micro-SMEs they won't touch. We publish pricing; they quote-gate.
competitors.ourGap: Deeper paid-media specialisation. Their ads team is larger than ours today.
competitors.battlecard: Closest head-to-head. Lean on Elitez pedigree + public pricing transparency. Ask: 'When was the last time an agency gave you monthly leads + CPL on a one-pager?'
objections: 2Stallions has stronger ads → true for big budgets; sub-$1K/mo ad spend, our bundled retainer wins on TCO

competitors.name: iFoundries
competitors.ourAdvantage: Retainer-native; campaign-hopping costs SMEs 2-3x per launch vs. ongoing retainer.
competitors.ourGap: Longer brand history and recognisable portfolio.
competitors.battlecard: Pitch recurring lead-gen vs. their campaign-hopping model. Cost per campaign × 4 campaigns > our annual retainer.
objections: iFoundries has been around longer → Elitez has been around 15yr; Pulse is the venture arm

competitors.name: Locomote Digital
competitors.ourAdvantage: SG price + SG sales presence; stronger buying signal for SG SMEs.
competitors.ourGap: Locomote has deeper MY local-market rolodex.
competitors.battlecard: For MY prospects, compete on our AI-augmented ops stack and group-backed overhead (no agency rent or senior-AM tax). For SG prospects, emphasise Locomote has no SG team / entity.
objections: Locomote is cheaper in MYR → sure, for MY-only campaigns; but SG billing + SG compliance costs you nothing extra with us

competitors.name: MediaOne Marketing
competitors.ourAdvantage: Fresher creative; video + design bundled; tighter reporting cadence.
competitors.ourGap: MediaOne owns SG SEO content engine at scale.
competitors.battlecard: If the prospect is SEO-obsessed, call out that Core already bundles SEO — not a separate upsell. Ask: 'What's your current content → leads conversion rate?'
objections: MediaOne has better SEO rankings → SEO is a 9-month game; we want your CPL down in 9 weeks

competitors.name: Fiverr Pro / Upwork
competitors.ourAdvantage: Structured retainer; single accountable vendor vs 4 freelancers with no SLAs.
competitors.ourGap: We are never going to be cheaper per deliverable.
competitors.battlecard: Reframe: you're not buying a design, you're buying a lead system. Marketplace freelancers cannot deliver that. Don't compete on unit price — change the unit.
objections: $50 Fiverr design → yes, for the logo alone; can they ship 20 posts + 4 videos + SEO + ads in the same month? No.

competitors.name: Freelance Singapore
competitors.ourAdvantage: Reliability, pipeline thinking, ROI tracking, SG entity for compliance-sensitive buyers.
competitors.ourGap: Their price floor is always lower per deliverable.
competitors.battlecard: If the prospect is serious about growth, move them off freelancer comparisons entirely. Sell the system, not the unit.
objections: Freelancers are cheaper → for one item, yes; for a lead-gen system, no

competitors.name: Naga DDB Tribal
competitors.ourAdvantage: Accessible — Naga is enterprise-only. Our price fits SME budgets they physically cannot serve.
competitors.ourGap: We don't compete on creative brand work at that scale.
competitors.battlecard: Not a real competitor for SMEs — only relevant if a prospect mentions them to signal ambition. Acknowledge their craft, redirect to why SME economics are different.
objections: Naga wins awards → you don't need awards, you need leads

competitors.name: GoDigital Academy
competitors.ourAdvantage: We produce AND run ads; they skew consulting/training.
competitors.battlecard: Their clients eventually graduate to needing execution — we are the upgrade path. Recommend them when prospect clearly wants training, not delivery.
objections: GoDigital will teach my team → great, teach them; come back when you want execution

competitors.name: Click Academy Asia
competitors.ourAdvantage: We bundle video + design; they are paid-media-only.
competitors.ourGap: Strong paid-media certifications on their side (Google Premier, Meta Business Partner).
competitors.battlecard: If prospect only wants ads, point to Premium + ads add-on — full retainer still wins on TCO once you include creative production.
objections: Click has Google Premier badge → fine for pure ads; we ship the creatives the ads run
